Notice Of Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, For A Protective Order; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $2,500
Off Calendar Feb-07-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Martin Zurada,Esq., VENARDI ZURADA LLP (925) 937-3900 Attorneys for Plaintiff ANDREW SCHER
Dpiglas Melton, Esq., LONG & LEVIT LLP (415) 397-2222 dmelton@longlevit.com Attorneys for Defendants HENRY WYKOWSKI and WYKOWSKI & WOOD, L.L.P.

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF ANDREW SCHER Notice Of Motion To Quash, Or Alternatively, For A Protective Order; Request For Sanctions In The Amount Of At Least $2,500

The Motion to Quash is granted except as follows: the subpoena shall be limited to any documents that refer to the client-related companies at issue in this case or mention that Andrew Scher owned shares of the Wykowski Law Firm's client-related companies.

Sanctions are awarded to Andrew Scher in the amount of $2,500.

Plaintiff's counsel to prepare and sumbit a proposed order tracking the court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312 to Judge Pro Tem Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Thomas Cohen; Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/JPT)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-27-2022 Continued To Mar-01-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT HENRY WYKOWSKI, WYKOWSKI & WOOD LLP'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant's demurrer to the amended complaint is overruled.

The court grants judicial notice of the adduced settlement agreement. The court, however, does not accept defendant's interpretation of the agreement as a matter not subject to dispute. (See Fremont Indemnity Co. v. Fremont General Corp. (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 97, 113 ["Taking judicial notice of a document is not the same as accepting the truth of its contents or accepting a particular interpretation of its meaning."]; Chacon v. Union Pacific Railroad (2020) 56 Cal.App.5th 565, 573 ["Although the existence of a document may be judicially noticeable, the truth of the statements contained in the document and its proper interpretation are not subject to judicial notice if those matters are reasonably disputable."]; Richteck USA, Inc. v. uPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.)

Here, the settlement agreement is ambiguous and it is disputed whether it applies to plaintiff's action. The agreement provides: "[b]y this Agreement, the Parties hereby set forth the terms on which they have agreed to resolve the Actions." Plaintiff's allegations, which the court accepts as true, that the settlement resolved the "actions," i.e., Dorgan I, II, III, and not the instant dispute, is reasonable and the court accepts that interpretation on demurrer. (See Aragon-Haas v. Family Security Ins. Services, Inc. (1991) 231 Cal.App.3d 232, 239 ["While plaintiffs' interpretation of the contract may prove invalid, it [is] improper to resolve the issue against her solely on her own pleading. 'In ruling on a demurrer, the likelihood that the pleading will be able to prove his allegations is not the question.'"]; Amended Complaint, pars. 18-19.) Defendant claims that no parol evidence can be introduced because the settlement was part of a mediation. The court cannot presently determine what parol evidence plaintiff will proffer and whether Evidence Code sec. 1119 will apply. Plaintiff further alleges that he rescinded the settlement, which raises another factual issue that the court cannot resolve on demurrer. (Amended Complaint, par. 23.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
